owasp_categories_covered: API3:2023 Broken Object Property Level Authorization
API6:2023 Unrestricted Access to Sensitive Business Flows
domain_risks_covered: OWASP Top 10 (generic domain - no domain-specific rules applied)

scenarios.title: BOLA: Access any user's post by enumerating post IDs
scenarios.description: An attacker can iterate over sequential /posts/:id values to read any user's post content without authentication. In a real system this would expose private content. Validates whether the API enforces ownership checks on resource access.
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /posts/1 through /posts/100 all return 200 - confirms no authorization boundary exists. A secure API would return 401/403 for resources not owned by the caller.

scenarios.title: BOLA: Enumerate all user PII via sequential ID traversal
scenarios.description: GET /users/:id exposes name, email, phone, address, and company for any user by ID. This is the highest-value BOLA target because user records contain PII (email, phone, physical address). An attacker with no credentials can harvest the entire user directory.
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /users/1 returns full user object with PII fields. HTTP 200 with complete address/phone/email confirms unrestricted PII access.

scenarios.title: Unauthenticated DELETE: Delete any post without credentials
scenarios.description: DELETE /posts/:id accepts requests with no authentication token. An attacker can destroy any user's content. This combines API2 (no auth) with API5 (function-level auth bypass on destructive operation).
scenarios.expected_outcome: DELETE /posts/1 returns 200 with empty body {}. Confirms destructive operations require zero authentication.

scenarios.title: Unauthenticated DELETE: Delete any user account without credentials
scenarios.description: DELETE /users/:id is the most destructive unauthenticated operation - it removes an entire user account. This is an admin-level action exposed without any auth or role check.
scenarios.expected_outcome: DELETE /users/1 returns 200 with {}. Confirms admin-level destructive action available to anonymous callers.

scenarios.title: Unrestricted resource consumption: GET /photos returns 5000 items unbounded
scenarios.description: GET /photos returns all 5000 photo records in a single response with no pagination. This is a denial-of-service vector - a single request forces the server to serialize 5000 objects and transmit a large payload. No rate limiting or pagination controls exist.
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /photos returns 200 with array of length 5000. Response size exceeds reasonable pagination limits. No Link/X-Total-Count headers present.

scenarios.title: SSRF via photo URL injection in POST /photos
scenarios.description: POST /photos accepts arbitrary url and thumbnailUrl string fields. An attacker can inject internal network URLs (http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/, http://localhost:8080/admin) that the server may fetch, enabling Server-Side Request Forgery to probe internal infrastructure.
scenarios.expected_outcome: POST /photos with url='http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/' returns 201 and stores the URL. Confirms no URL validation or allowlist enforcement on user-supplied URLs.

scenarios.title: Mass assignment: Create post with arbitrary userId impersonation
scenarios.description: POST /posts accepts any userId value in the request body. An attacker can create posts attributed to any user, impersonating them. No server-side override of userId from auth context.
scenarios.owasp_category: API3:2023 Broken Object Property Level Authorization
scenarios.expected_outcome: POST /posts with userId=999 returns 201 with the attacker-supplied userId preserved in the response.

scenarios.title: BOLA via PUT: Replace another user's post entirely
scenarios.description: PUT /posts/:id allows full replacement of any post without verifying the caller owns it. Combined with userId spoofing, an attacker can reassign ownership of any post.
scenarios.expected_outcome: PUT /posts/1 with userId=99 returns 200 with the new userId. Confirms ownership transfer without auth.

scenarios.title: BOLA via PATCH: Modify another user's post title
scenarios.description: PATCH /posts/:id accepts partial updates to any post without ownership verification. An attacker can silently alter content across the platform.
scenarios.expected_outcome: PATCH /posts/1 with {title: 'Hacked'} returns 200 with modified title. No 401/403.

scenarios.title: BOLA via PUT: Replace another user's profile data
scenarios.description: PUT /users/:id replaces any user's profile (name, email, phone) without authentication. This enables account takeover by overwriting contact information.
scenarios.expected_outcome: PUT /users/1 with attacker-supplied email returns 200 with new email. Profile overwritten without auth.

scenarios.title: BOLA via PATCH: Modify another user's email address
scenarios.description: PATCH /users/:id allows modifying PII fields (email, phone) of any user. This is an account takeover vector if the email is used for password reset flows.
scenarios.expected_outcome: PATCH /users/1 with {email: 'attacker@evil.com'} returns 200 with modified email.

scenarios.title: Mass assignment: Create comment with spoofed email
scenarios.description: POST /comments accepts an arbitrary email field. An attacker can post comments impersonating any email address, enabling spam or phishing attribution.
scenarios.owasp_category: API3:2023 Broken Object Property Level Authorization
scenarios.expected_outcome: POST /comments with email='ceo@company.com' returns 201 with that email in the response.

scenarios.title: BOLA via PUT: Replace another user's comment
scenarios.description: PUT /comments/:id replaces any comment without auth. An attacker can alter comment content or reassign postId to move comments between posts.
scenarios.expected_outcome: PUT /comments/1 with modified body returns 200. Comment content overwritten without auth.

scenarios.title: Unauthenticated DELETE: Delete any comment
scenarios.description: DELETE /comments/:id removes comments without authentication. Enables content censorship by anonymous actors.
scenarios.expected_outcome: DELETE /comments/1 returns 200 with {}.

scenarios.title: Unauthenticated DELETE: Delete any album
scenarios.description: DELETE /albums/:id removes albums (and implicitly their photos) without authentication.
scenarios.expected_outcome: DELETE /albums/1 returns 200 with {}.

scenarios.title: Unauthenticated DELETE: Delete any photo
scenarios.description: DELETE /photos/:id removes photo records without authentication.
scenarios.expected_outcome: DELETE /photos/1 returns 200 with {}.

scenarios.title: Unauthenticated DELETE: Delete any todo
scenarios.description: DELETE /todos/:id removes todo items without authentication. Enables sabotage of another user's task list.
scenarios.expected_outcome: DELETE /todos/1 returns 200 with {}.

scenarios.title: No rate limiting on GET /posts list endpoint
scenarios.description: GET /posts has no rate limiting. An attacker can issue thousands of requests per second to scrape all data or cause resource exhaustion.
scenarios.expected_outcome: 100 rapid GET /posts requests all return 200 with no 429 responses. Confirms absence of rate limiting.

scenarios.title: Bulk resource creation flood: POST /posts with no rate limit
scenarios.description: POST /posts accepts unlimited creation requests. An attacker can flood the system with garbage posts. This tests API6 (unrestricted access to sensitive business flows - content creation).
scenarios.owasp_category: API6:2023 Unrestricted Access to Sensitive Business Flows
scenarios.expected_outcome: 50 sequential POST /posts requests all return 201 with incrementing IDs. No throttling or CAPTCHA challenge.

scenarios.title: SSRF via PUT: Replace photo URL with internal network target
scenarios.description: PUT /photos/:id allows replacing url/thumbnailUrl with attacker-controlled internal URLs. If the server fetches these for thumbnail generation, internal services are exposed.
scenarios.expected_outcome: PUT /photos/1 with url='http://localhost:3000/admin' returns 200. Internal URL accepted without validation.

scenarios.title: Mass assignment: Create todo for another user
scenarios.description: POST /todos accepts arbitrary userId. An attacker can add tasks to any user's todo list, causing confusion or social engineering.
scenarios.owasp_category: API3:2023 Broken Object Property Level Authorization
scenarios.expected_outcome: POST /todos with userId=5 returns 201 with that userId preserved.

scenarios.title: Mass assignment: Create album attributed to another user
scenarios.description: POST /albums accepts arbitrary userId. An attacker can create albums attributed to any user.
scenarios.owasp_category: API3:2023 Broken Object Property Level Authorization
scenarios.expected_outcome: POST /albums with userId=5 returns 201 with that userId preserved.

scenarios.title: Unauthenticated user account creation
scenarios.description: POST /users creates new user accounts with no authentication. An attacker can flood the system with fake accounts.
scenarios.owasp_category: API6:2023 Unrestricted Access to Sensitive Business Flows
scenarios.expected_outcome: POST /users returns 201 with new user ID. No auth, CAPTCHA, or email verification required.

scenarios.title: BOLA via PATCH: Modify another user's todo completion status
scenarios.description: PATCH /todos/:id allows toggling completed status on any user's todo without auth. Enables sabotage of task tracking.
scenarios.expected_outcome: PATCH /todos/1 with {completed: true} returns 200 with modified state.

scenarios.title: Missing security headers on API responses
scenarios.description: Verify API responses include security headers: X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Content-Security-Policy. Missing headers enable clickjacking, MIME sniffing, and downgrade attacks.
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /posts response headers checked. Missing headers documented. Expect at minimum X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff.

scenarios.title: Overly permissive CORS configuration
scenarios.description: Check Access-Control-Allow-Origin header. A wildcard (*) CORS policy allows any website to make authenticated API requests on behalf of users, enabling cross-origin data theft.
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /posts with Origin header returns Access-Control-Allow-Origin. Document whether it's * (permissive) or restricted.

scenarios.title: Boundary: GET /posts/0 - zero ID handling
scenarios.description: Test how the API handles ID=0, which is often a boundary error. Some ORMs treat 0 as null/falsy, potentially returning wrong data or causing server errors.
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /posts/0 returns 404 with empty object {}.

scenarios.title: Boundary: GET /posts/-1 - negative ID handling
scenarios.description: Negative IDs may bypass unsigned integer validation and reach database queries with unexpected values, potentially causing SQL errors or returning unintended data.
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /posts/-1 returns 404.

scenarios.title: Non-numeric ID: GET /posts/abc - string injection in path parameter
scenarios.description: String values in numeric ID parameters may cause type coercion errors, expose stack traces, or bypass input validation. Tests path parameter type safety.
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /posts/abc returns 404. Must NOT return 500 or expose stack trace.

scenarios.title: Overflow ID: GET /posts/999999999999 - integer overflow
scenarios.description: Extremely large IDs may cause integer overflow in database queries or ORM layers, potentially returning wrong records or crashing.
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /posts/999999999999 returns 404. Must NOT return 500.

scenarios.title: Empty body POST /posts - missing required fields
scenarios.description: POST /posts with empty JSON body {} tests server-side validation. A well-implemented API should reject requests missing required fields.
scenarios.expected_outcome: POST /posts with {} should return 400/422 with validation error. JSONPlaceholder returns 201 (documents the gap).

scenarios.title: XSS payload in post title field
scenarios.description: POST /posts with title containing <script>alert('xss')</script> tests whether the API stores and returns unsanitized HTML. If rendered in a frontend, this enables stored XSS.
scenarios.expected_outcome: POST /posts with XSS payload returns 201. Check if script tags are stored verbatim or sanitized in response body.

scenarios.title: Wrong type: POST /posts with userId as string
scenarios.description: POST /posts with userId='not_a_number' tests type coercion. Weak validation may accept string values for integer fields, leading to data integrity issues.
scenarios.owasp_category: API3:2023 Broken Object Property Level Authorization
scenarios.expected_outcome: POST /posts with userId='abc' should return 400/422. Document actual behavior.

scenarios.title: GET /comments returns 500 items without pagination
scenarios.description: GET /comments returns all 500 comments in a single response. Tests pagination absence and response size impact.
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /comments returns 200 with array of length 500. No pagination metadata.

scenarios.title: GET /todos returns 200 items without pagination
scenarios.description: GET /todos returns all 200 items. While smaller than /photos, still validates pagination absence.
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /todos returns 200 with array of length 200.

scenarios.title: PUT /posts/:id with extra unknown fields - mass assignment probe
scenarios.description: PUT /posts/1 with extra fields (isAdmin: true, role: 'admin') tests whether the API silently accepts and stores unexpected properties. This is a classic mass assignment vector.
scenarios.owasp_category: API3:2023 Broken Object Property Level Authorization
scenarios.expected_outcome: PUT /posts/1 with {isAdmin: true, ...} - check if extra fields appear in response.

scenarios.title: Nested BOLA: Access any post's comments via /posts/:id/comments
scenarios.description: GET /posts/:id/comments leaks all comments for any post without auth. Attacker can enumerate post IDs and harvest all associated comments including email addresses.
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /posts/1/comments returns 200 with array of comments containing email fields.

scenarios.title: Nested BOLA: Enumerate any user's posts via /users/:id/posts
scenarios.description: GET /users/:id/posts exposes all posts for any user without auth. Enables targeted content scraping per user.
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /users/1/posts returns 200 with all posts by user 1.

scenarios.title: Nested BOLA: Enumerate any user's todos via /users/:id/todos
scenarios.description: GET /users/:id/todos exposes private task lists without auth. Reveals personal productivity data.
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /users/1/todos returns 200 with all todos for user 1.

scenarios.title: Undocumented HTTP methods: OPTIONS/HEAD/TRACE on /posts
scenarios.description: Test whether undocumented HTTP methods are enabled. TRACE enables XST (cross-site tracing). OPTIONS may reveal internal routing. Tests API9 (improper inventory management).
scenarios.expected_outcome: OPTIONS /posts - document allowed methods. TRACE /posts should return 405. HEAD /posts returns headers only.

scenarios.title: Email field injection in POST /comments
scenarios.description: POST /comments with email containing special chars or CRLF injection (email: 'test@x.com\r\nBcc: victim@x.com') tests for header injection if email is used in notifications.
scenarios.expected_outcome: POST /comments with injected email returns 201. Check if CRLF characters are stored verbatim.

scenarios.title: Verify user PII fields exposed: email, phone, address in response
scenarios.description: GET /users/:id returns full PII without field-level access control. In production, sensitive fields (phone, address.geo) should be filtered based on caller authorization level.
scenarios.owasp_category: API3:2023 Broken Object Property Level Authorization
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /users/1 returns object with email, phone, address.street, address.geo.lat/lng - all PII exposed.

scenarios.title: BOLA via PUT: Replace another user's album
scenarios.description: PUT /albums/:id replaces any album without auth. Can reassign ownership via userId field.
scenarios.expected_outcome: PUT /albums/1 with userId=99 returns 200 with new userId.

scenarios.title: BOLA via PUT: Replace another user's todo
scenarios.description: PUT /todos/:id replaces any todo without auth. Can mark others' tasks complete or reassign ownership.
scenarios.expected_outcome: PUT /todos/1 with userId=99 returns 200 with new userId.

scenarios.title: SSRF via PATCH: Inject internal URL into photo thumbnailUrl
scenarios.description: PATCH /photos/:id with thumbnailUrl pointing to internal service. Tests partial update SSRF vector.
scenarios.expected_outcome: PATCH /photos/1 with {thumbnailUrl: 'http://10.0.0.1:8080/'} returns 200. Internal URL accepted.

scenarios.title: Oversized payload: POST /posts with 1MB body text
scenarios.description: POST /posts with a body field containing ~1MB of text tests request size limits. Absence of limits enables storage exhaustion attacks.
scenarios.expected_outcome: POST /posts with large body - check if 413 Payload Too Large or 201 accepted. Document threshold.

scenarios.title: Bulk comment creation flood: POST /comments with no rate limit
scenarios.description: Rapid POST /comments to test comment spam protection. No rate limit means bots can flood comment sections.
scenarios.owasp_category: API6:2023 Unrestricted Access to Sensitive Business Flows
scenarios.expected_outcome: 20 rapid POST /comments all return 201. No 429 or CAPTCHA.

scenarios.title: Nested endpoint with nonexistent parent: GET /posts/999999/comments
scenarios.description: Test behavior when parent resource doesn't exist. JSONPlaceholder returns 200 with []. A strict API should return 404 for nonexistent parent.
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /posts/999999/comments returns 200 with []. Documents lenient parent validation.

scenarios.title: Contract: GET /posts returns array of valid post objects
scenarios.description: Baseline contract test. Verify GET /posts returns 200 with an array where each item matches the post schema (userId, id, title, body - all required).
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /posts returns 200. Response is array of 100 items. Each matches post schema.

scenarios.title: Contract: GET /posts/1 returns single valid post
scenarios.description: Verify GET /posts/1 returns 200 with a single post object matching schema and id=1.
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /posts/1 returns 200 with {userId: int, id: 1, title: string, body: string}.

scenarios.title: Contract: GET /comments returns array of valid comment objects
scenarios.description: Verify GET /comments returns 200 with 500 items matching comment schema.
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /comments returns 200. Array of 500 items with postId, id, name, email, body.

scenarios.title: Contract: GET /albums returns array of valid album objects
scenarios.description: Verify GET /albums returns 200 with 100 items matching album schema.
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /albums returns 200. Array of 100 items with userId, id, title.

scenarios.title: Contract: GET /photos returns array of valid photo objects
scenarios.description: Verify GET /photos returns 200 with 5000 items matching photo schema.
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /photos returns 200. Array of 5000 items with albumId, id, title, url, thumbnailUrl.

scenarios.title: Contract: GET /todos returns array of valid todo objects
scenarios.description: Verify GET /todos returns 200 with 200 items matching todo schema.
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /todos returns 200. Array of 200 items with userId, id, title, completed.

scenarios.title: Contract: GET /users returns array of 10 valid user objects
scenarios.description: Verify GET /users returns 200 with exactly 10 users matching full user schema including nested address and company.
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /users returns 200. Array of 10 items with complete nested structure.

scenarios.title: Contract: GET /posts/1/comments returns filtered comments
scenarios.description: Verify nested endpoint returns comments where all have postId=1.
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /posts/1/comments returns 200 with 5 comments, all with postId=1.

scenarios.title: Contract: GET /albums/1/photos returns filtered photos
scenarios.description: Verify nested endpoint returns photos where all have albumId=1.
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /albums/1/photos returns 200 with 50 photos, all with albumId=1.

scenarios.title: Query filter: GET /posts?userId=1 returns only user 1 posts
scenarios.description: Verify query parameter filtering correctly restricts results by userId.
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /posts?userId=1 returns 200 with array where every item has userId=1.

scenarios.title: Nested vs query consistency: /posts/1/comments matches /comments?postId=1
scenarios.description: The nested route and query filter should return identical result sets. Inconsistency indicates a routing or filtering bug.
scenarios.expected_outcome: Both endpoints return same set of comment IDs.

scenarios.title: Response time SLA: GET /posts completes under 2 seconds
scenarios.description: Baseline performance check for the most common read operation. Establishes latency SLA.
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /posts responds in < 2000ms. Document actual p50/p95 latency.

scenarios.title: Response time SLA: GET /photos (5000 items) under 5 seconds
scenarios.description: Performance test for the largest payload endpoint. Measures serialization and transfer time for 5000 records.
scenarios.expected_outcome: GET /photos responds in < 5000ms. Document actual latency and payload size.

scenarios.title: HTTP method probing: PUT/PATCH/DELETE on collection endpoint /posts
scenarios.description: Test whether bulk destructive operations are possible on collection endpoints. PUT /posts or DELETE /posts could affect all records if not properly guarded.
scenarios.expected_outcome: PUT /posts, PATCH /posts, DELETE /posts all return 404 or 405 Method Not Allowed.
